channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO shook when police confronted him, officers say
item.description: ALTOONA, Pa. (AP) — Police arrested a suspect Monday in the brazen Manhattan killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO after a quick-thinking McDonald’s customer in Pennsylvania spotted a man who officers found with a gun, mask and writings linking him to the ambush.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, MARK SCOLFORO and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden's approval of an oil lease sale will keep the door open to drilling in Alaska's Arctic refuge
item.description: The Biden administration has given approval for the sale of oil and gas leases in Alaska, leaving the door open for drilling in a portion of the pristine Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.
item.dc:creator: Becky Bohrer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Polygamous sect leader gets 50 years in prison in scheme to orchestrate sex involving children
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A polygamist religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives” including 10 underage girls was sentenced to 50 years in prison on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults, and for scheming to kidnap them from
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A gang leader in Haiti is accused of massacring older people to avenge his son's death
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — A gang leader who controls a key port in Haiti's capital is accused of massacring older people and Vodou religious leaders in his community to avenge his son's death, according to the government and human rights organizations that estimate more than 100 were killed.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New Jersey becomes latest state to prohibit bans on books in school, public libraries
item.description: PRINCETON, N.J. (AP) — New Jersey Democratic Gov. Phil Murphy signed a law Monday to prohibit public and school libraries from banning books in the state and to enshrine protections against civil and criminal charges for librarians who comply with the law.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 10 new TV shows from 2024 that are worth watching before the new year
item.description: It could be argued that 2024 wasn't the best year for television. Highly anticipated shows like the final season of “Stranger Things," “The Last of Us” and “The White Lotus” didn't even make it on the calendar and will instead return in 2025.
item.dc:creator: ALICIA RANCILIO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Key details to know about the arrest of a suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: Police arrested a 26-year-old man on Monday in the Manhattan killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO after they say a Pennsylvania McDonald's worker alerted authorities to a customer who resembled the suspected gunman.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 6 Guatemalans arrested and charged with human smuggling in deadly 2021 Mexico truck crash
item.description: United States and Guatemalan authorities have announced the arrest of six people charged with human smuggling linked to a semi-trailer truck crash in Mexico in 2021 that killed more than 50 migrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO was likely killed with a ghost gun that can be made at home
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The brazen killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO was likely carried out with a ghost gun, one of the nearly untraceable weapons that can be made a home, police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rupert Murdoch loses bid to change family trust
item.description: Murdoch, 93, late last year ceded his position as executive chairman of Fox and News Corp to Lachlan, who now presides over a sprawling media empire.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans pick Mast to lead House Foreign Affairs panel
item.description: House Republicans selected Rep. Brian Mast (R-Fla.) to chair the House Foreign Affairs Committee in the 119th Congress, sources told The Hill, as the conference works to organize its panels for the next two years of Republican-controlled Washington Republicans also selected Rep.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Who is Luigi Mangione? Police arrest person of interest in CEO killing
item.description: A man arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson has been identified as Luigi Mangione, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, KRON4

item.title: Trump’s vow to end birthright citizenship could face legal challenges, GOP skeptics
item.description: President-elect Trump has doubled down on his vow to end birthright citizenship, a task that would likely face legal challenges as well as skepticism from within his own party. For starters, the 14th Amendment grants citizenship to those born in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Petition urges food allergy measures after Disney influencer's death
item.description: A Change.org petition calling on more food allergy measures in California has been launched after influencer Dominique Brown passed away after experiencing an allergic reaction to holiday food served at an event in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok asks federal appeals court to bar enforcement of potential ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: TikTok asked a federal appeals court on Monday to bar the Biden administration from enforcing a law that could lead to a ban on the popular platform until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge to the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Daniel Penny acquitted in NYC subway chokehold case over Jordan Neely's death
item.description: A Marine veteran who used a chokehold on an agitated subway rider was acquitted on Monday in a death that became a prism for differing views about public safety, valor and vigilantism.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Daniel Penny found not guilty in subway chokehold trial
item.description: A Manhattan jury has reached its verdict in the Daniel Penny's trial in the death of Jordan Neely, who died after a May 2023 subway chokehold.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gas prices drop to lowest in 3 years, AAA says
item.description: (KRON) -- Good news for the millions of drivers across American roads: gas prices are falling. The national average for a gallon of gas has hit a three-year low, the American Automobile Association (AAA) recently announced.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American woman faces prison time in Europe for fatally stabbing migrant man who allegedly assaulted her
item.description: A 20-year-old American woman has been accused of fatally stabbing a migrant man in Germany, who she said sexually assaulted her at a train station, leading to the stabbing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYPD believes UnitedHealthcare CEO assassin left New York City on a bus morning of shooting
item.description: Brian Thompson's suspected killer left the scene using a variety of transportation methods and is expected to have fled New York City within an hour.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gas prices drop to lowest in 3 years, AAA says
item.description: Good news for the millions of drivers on American roads: gas prices are falling. The national average for a gallon of gas has hit a three-year low, the American Automobile Association (AAA) recently announced.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, Fox 5

item.title: Daniel Penny jury set to deliberate lesser charge after manslaughter deadlock and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scottie Scheffler ends his big year in the Bahamas with his 9th victory
item.description:
Scottie Scheffler returned from a two-month break and nothing changed. He won the Hero World Challenge on Sunday with a 9-under 63 to tie the tournament record at Albany Golf Club and win by six shots.
Scheffler, the first player since Tiger Woods in 2009 to spend an entire calendar year at No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ousted Syrian leader Assad flees to Moscow, Russian state media say
item.description: Syrians poured into streets echoing with celebratory gunfire on Sunday after a stunning rebel advance reached the capital, putting an end to the Assad family's 50 years of iron rule but raising questions about the future of the country and the wider region.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump calls for 'immediate ceasefire' in Ukraine after meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump claimed that Moscow and Kyiv have both lost hundreds of thousands of soldiers in a war that “should never have started.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New photos released of suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: The manhunt continues for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson’s killer as police released new photos of the suspect.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, KRON4

item.title: Ousted Syrian leader Assad flees to Moscow, Russian state media say
item.description: Syrians poured into streets echoing with celebratory gunfire on Sunday after a stunning rebel advance reached the capital, putting an end to the Assad family's 50 years of iron rule but raising questions about the future of the country and the wider region.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Notre Dame reopens its doors to Macron and other world leaders in a rare symbol of unity
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, KTLA 5

item.title: Jokic's career high of 56 points comes during a low point for the Nuggets
item.description: Nikola Jokic scored a career-high 56 points and had little to celebrate afterward. It happened in a loss — to a team that hadn't won since October.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Doorbell camera captures Illinois garbage truck explosion
item.description: Authorities described the explosion as "significant" and said truck parts were launched into the air, landing several blocks away.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, Fox 5

item.title: Stolen ruby slippers worn by Judy Garland in 'The Wizard of Oz' are auctioned for $28 million
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A pair of iconic ruby slippers that were worn by Judy Garland in “The Wizard of Oz” and stolen from a museum nearly two decades ago sold for a winning bid of $28 million at auction Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI and HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man on trial in the killing of an Ole Miss student gave conflicting information, police say
item.description: The man on trial in the killing of University of Mississippi student Jimmie “Jay” Lee gave conflicting information to police about how well he and Lee knew each other, according to testimony Friday by an officer who helped lead the investigation.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Recordings: Woman says slain Kentucky judge was involved in alleged sextortion scandal
item.description: In the recording, a woman claims to have seen video of Mullins engaging in sexual acts in his office.
item.dc:creator: Allison Hope Weiner, Fox 5

item.title: In California's 'earthquake country,' a 7.0 temblor prompts confusion and a tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Valerie Starkey was driving through Northern California to visit relatives when she suddenly felt shaking and feared her car had broken down, only to realize later that it was an earthquake so powerful that it triggered a tsunami warning for hundreds of miles of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Splash pads associated with waterborne disease outbreaks: CDC
item.description: "Children are at increased risk for exposure to pathogens in contaminated splash pad water," the CDC said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, ABC 4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing spotlights challenge of protecting leaders of companies
item.description: He’s one of the most famous and widely admired corporate leaders in the world. But it’s the haters that companies like Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta worry about.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
